relations.note: The geometry proportions govern how the callout box components mapped in the main rule are sized and positioned.

quotes: box border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;
border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;
box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);
Light Blue `#E6F0F9` Box fill
A light-blue accent-shape box containing a short blue headline, with a white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge
Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape.
Icon badge ? a White `#ffffff` rounded square holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke), vertically centered on the box and horizontally centered on the box's left edge
box border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px; in TL TR BR BL order  large on top-left + bottom-right, near-point on top-right + bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio.
The white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale, border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px; (also 5:1, large TL+BR).
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.
the white icon badge must carry a subtle drop shadow so it lifts off the blue box  e.g. box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

rules.intent: Maintain the distinct geometric framing, styling, and drop shadow of the iconic brand callout container.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout Box uses a Light Blue fill (color.tertiary.light_blue = #E6F0F9) with an asymmetric 5:1 corner radius (radius.callout.icon_box_container = 40px 8px 40px 8px). It features a White icon badge centered on its left edge that mirrors the 5:1 radius shape at a smaller scale (radius.callout.icon_badge_container = 20px 4px 20px 4px) and carries a mandatory subtle drop shadow (shadow.callout.badge = 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18)) to lift it off the box.
rules.snippets: These improvements occurred as early as four weeks after starting treatment, and they continued getting better through the end of the 52-week study.

quotes: <div style="position:relative; padding-left:40px;">
<td style="background-color:#E6F0F9; border-radius:40px 8px 40px 8px; padding:26px 34px 26px 64px;">

<div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18); text-align:center; line-height:80px;">
The blue box itself is inset from the left by half the badge width so the badge's outer half has room
left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap (? W/2 + 24px).
Never let copy slide under the badge.
Let W = badge width (use 80px, icon ?48px inside).

Inset the blue box from the left by W/2 = 40px, and place the badge so its center lands exactly on the box's left edge (badge left:0 inside a padding-left:40px wrapper ? badge center 40px = box edge). Distance from badge center to box edge must be 0.
Defaults: badge size W = 80px, inner icon 48px.
If your real badge is a different size, keep the relationships  inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge margin-top = ?H/2  and the bisector stays exact.

rules.intent: Maintain pixel-perfect horizontal and vertical alignment ratios of the overlapping layout.
rules.rule_text: The icon callout box layout requires precise geometrical proportional relationships based on the badge width 'W' (default 80px). The blue box must be inset from the left by exactly W/2 (40px) to give the badge's outer half room, and the badge center must align exactly on the box's left edge (distance to edge = 0). The box's internal left-padding must clear the badge with a gap calculated as W/2 + 24px (64px for W=80px) to prevent copy from sliding under the badge.
